with.style: clean technical architecture diagram, vector-like, precise, modern systems design
with.prompt: Create a polished technical architecture diagram titled 'Quasar session repository: corpus-scale path'. Use a light neutral background, crisp dark text, restrained accents in teal, blue, amber, and red. Make labels large and legible. Show left-to-right flow: 1) Agent history sources: Codex, OpenCode, Claude Code, Hermes, Cursor, JSONL, SQLite snapshots. 2) Quasar CLI plus core adapters: source safety snapshots, SQLite copy-before-read, JSONL read-only. 3) Bulk ingest API: start import job, submit bounded chunks. 4) Convex durable state: importJobs, importChunks, importShards, importCheckpoints, importFailures. 5) Worker lane: lease-token claim, bounded mutation, retry, dead letter, completion proof. 6) Repository graph: sessions, events, tool calls, content blocks, edges, usage, artifacts. 7) Search layer: lexical documents, embedding outbox, readiness aggregates. 8) Dashboard: job progress, failures, paginated session explorer, search diagnostics. Include a bottom guardrail strip: 'source histories read-only', 'Effect Schema boundaries', 'no raw tool-output embeddings', 'cursor pagination'. Use simple boxes and arrows, no decorative 3D objects, no tiny paragraphs.

with.style: clean technical flowchart, vector-like, operational infrastructure diagram
with.prompt: Create a technical flowchart titled 'Quasar bulk ingest lifecycle'. Use large readable labels and a sober operations palette: charcoal text, white cards, teal success, amber retry, red dead-letter, blue queue arrows. Show a numbered sequence: 1 Start job with Effect Schema decoded input. 2 Store importJob with stable idempotency key and immutable expectedChunkCount. 3 Enqueue chunks with non-negative integer sequence and positive integer expectedChunkCount. 4 Claim next due chunk with leaseToken and leaseExpiresAt. 5 Process chunk through bounded Convex mutation: sessions, events, tool calls, graph rows, search docs, checkpoints. 6 Mark success only if lease token matches; stale completions no-op. 7 On failure, retry with backoff until terminal dead_letter. 8 Finalize only when uploaded count, terminal count, and terminal sequence sum prove contiguous 0..N-1 completion. 9 Dashboard shows partial state, succeeded chunks, failed chunks, readiness. Add side callouts: 'CLI can exit after upload', 'reruns converge without duplicate terminal accounting', 'source safety report before/after'. No small text blocks; keep the diagram inspectable.

with.style: clean data policy architecture diagram, vector-like, clear pipeline with decision gates
with.prompt: Create a technical architecture diagram titled 'Quasar embedding policy and readiness'. Make labels large and legible. Show source rows entering 'search document builder', then split into two lanes. Lane A: lexical search text stores searchable metadata and summaries for sessions, events, tool calls, content blocks, artifacts. Lane B: embedding text gate. Decision gate: eligible only for narrative session summaries, user messages, and final assistant responses. Explicit red stop signs for tool calls, tool results, raw content blocks, artifacts, code/file read outputs: captured in storage but skipped for embeddings. Then show embeddingOutbox with pause/resume/retry, lease-controlled drain, provider call, scoped cache key, embeddingCache, RAG entry, readiness aggregates by job/project/provider/family/state, search diagnostics for text/semantic/fusion. Include status chips: pending, syncing, ready, skipped, failed, dead_letter. Add bottom warning: 'do not accidentally embed repeated codebase/tool outputs'. Use clean arrows, concise labels, and no dense prose.
